Caption: Main structure of the methodology employed in this paper. Details of each block are explained in depth in its respective methodology section.
GP tree-structured chromosome for the mathematical function X 0 e X 1 + 2 .

Heatmap showing the correlations (Pearson coefficient) between every variable considered in this study. Notice that the conductivity was calculated as an output of the mechanistic models, while all the other variables were generated by the LHS method and used as inputs.

Parity plot comparing the simulated conductivity using the mechanistic models vs. the conductivity predicted by (a) the NR-F neural network and (b) the WR-F neural network. All data were linearly normalized using the training dataset - i.e. the “Min-Max” function. The parity line, where the predicted values are in perfect agreement with the simulated ones are displayed, as well as two auxiliary dotted lines highlighting all the samples that either succeeded or failed in predicting the results with a relative error higher than 10%. Image expansions were placed in the bottom right to showcase zones where the data visualization may be challenging.

Caption: Relative errors comparing the diffusion coefficients used in the mechanistic models vs. the diffusion coefficients predicted by (a) ionic diffusion, by the NR-B neural network; (b) electronic diffusion, by the NR-B neural network; (c) ionic diffusion, by the WR-B neural network; and (d) electronic diffusion, by the WR-B neural network.
